of service would be considered reasonable and necessary. However, where there are 
complications that require more frequent skilled care related to the catheter, such care 
would, with adequate documentation, be covered. 

EXAMPLE: A patient who has a Foley catheter due to loss of bladder control because 
of multiple sclerosis has a history of frequent plugging of the catheter and urinary tract
